transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yes.
What is your main medical concern today?
I have knee pain.
When did your knee pain start?
About 2 hours ago.
Can you describe what caused your knee pain or if you injured it?
I injured it while walking my dog.
Can you describe the type of pain you are feeling in your knee and if there is any swelling or difficulty moving it?
It's very sharp when I try to put weight on it.
Is there any visible swelling, bruising, or deformity of the knee?
There is a little swelling on the of the knee and some bruising around it too.
Are you able to bend or straighten your knee, or is movement severely limited?
I'm able to bend and straighten it. I just hurts a lot when I put any weight on it.
Any numbness, tingling, or weakness in your leg or foot?
No.
Any fever, chills, or redness spreading around the knee?
No.
Have you tried any treatments or taken any medications for your knee pain so far?
No. I haven't done anything yet.
Can you describe exactly where on your knee the pain is located?
Below my kneecap, on the right side.
Any history of previous knee injuries or surgeries on this knee?
No.
The next few questions may feel repetitive, but they are important for medication safety and treatment decisions. How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
Penicillin.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
Female.
Is pregnancy possible? (yes/no/unknown)
No.
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No.

It sounds like you have an acute knee injury likely caused by a bruise or soft tissue damage from your recent fall. Because I can’t examine you or do any tests over the phone, a licensed physician will review your information and may recommend medication like ibuprofen or acetaminophen to help with pain. For now, try to rest your knee and avoid putting too much weight on it if it hurts. If you notice your knee swelling more, becoming deformed, you can’t put weight on it, or if you develop a fever, please seek in-person care the same day. If the pain gets much worse or you can’t move your knee at all, go to the emergency room right away. A doctor may want to see you in person for an exam or imaging if your symptoms don’t improve in the next several days.
